sections.opening: Come sit, dear soul. The candle is lit and time is open to wonder. In the previous lesson we delved into psalms of new beginnings — Psalm 1 for right choices, Psalm 2 for protection from enemies, Psalm 3 for trust, Psalm 4 for repentance and Psalm 5 for a morning prayer that awakens salvation. Now, as we surrender more to the power of Psalms, we discover that each chapter carries within it an entire world of protection and healing. Today we will open together the healing gates of Psalms — chapters whose power heals eyes and soul, heals sick children, and protects us from all evil. This is our heavenly pharmacy, beloved. Come, let us enter inside.
sections.theoryA:
How Do Psalms Heal Eyes and Illness?

Psalm 6 is called "The Great Healer" — it is the gateway to healing eye diseases and alleviating all distress from the soul. Tradition teaches us that when King David said "Have mercy on me, O Lord, for I am weak; heal me, O Lord, for my bones are troubled," he opened a healing pathway from heaven to earth. This psalm works on multiple levels: physically it treats the physical eyes, and spiritually it heals the "inner eyes" — the ability to see good in life.

Psalm 9 was transmitted as a special remedy for healing a sick child. The tradition of the Ashkenazi sages tells that mothers would say this chapter while weeping over the sick child's bed, and the Holy One sent healing. The chapter begins with "I will give thanks to the Lord with my whole heart" — such gratitude that comes from the depth of the heart opens gates of mercy. This is not ordinary healing, this is a chain reaction that begins in the mother's heart and reaches the throne of glory.

Psalm 41 is the general healing — "Blessed is he who considers the poor; the Lord will deliver him in the day of trouble." The sages explained that one who understands the secret of giving and compassion merits that the Holy One will have compassion on them in times of trouble. This is a cosmic principle: kindness creates kindness, compassion brings mercy. When you care for the sick, you open your own healing power.

practice1.instruction: Take a paper and write the names of three people whose health you care about. Next to each name write which chapter is appropriate: Psalm 6 for eyes and soul, Psalm 9 for children, Psalm 41 for general healing. Now read aloud the first verse of each chapter and imagine the words touching the sick person like warm light.

sections.theoryB:
How Do Psalms Protect from All Evil and Danger?

Chapter 18 is the giant among the protection psalms — "I love you, O Lord, my strength. The Lord is my rock and my fortress and my deliverer." This is a psalm of salvation from harsh decrees and from enemies. King David is said to have recited this psalm after he was saved from the hand of Saul and from all enemies, and he brought the intensity of this experience into the words. When you feel attacked or in danger, chapter 18 wraps you in an invisible shield.

Chapter 31 gives us the deep secret of trust — "Into your hand I commit my spirit." This is the chapter that protects from evil eye and evil tongue, but also from the toxic feeling of lack of control. When you say "Into your hand I commit my spirit" — you release the soul from the struggle to control everything, and transfer the responsibility to the one who truly runs the world. This is like spiritual yoga that immediately brings relief.

Chapter 3 teaches us how not to fear — "The Lord is a shield about me, my glory and the lifter of my head." Kabbalistic sages explain that there are fears that come from the past and fears that come from the future. Chapter 3 deals with both — it reminds the soul that there is someone who always protects it, and this gives it strength to face whatever comes. This is not denial of reality, this is remembering the deep truth.

The connection to other spiritual worlds is wondrous: in Kabbalah, these chapters parallel the protective sefirot — Chesed, Gevurah and Tiferet. In astrology, they connect to the protective forces of the Sun (vitality), Mars (strength to fight) and Jupiter (natural protection). In Tarot, they parallel the strength cards — The Tower that brings down what is not good, the inner strength that helps cope, and The Star that brings hope after the storm.

practice2.instruction: Choose a situation in your life where you feel insecurity or fear. Write it in one sentence. Now read aloud the verse "The Lord is a shield about me, my glory and the lifter of my head" (Psalm 3:4) five times, and each time imagine a deep and confident voice saying this directly into your fear. Feel how the words change something in the feeling in your stomach.

sections.deepDive:
What's Not Written in Books: Body Mapping to Psalms

Many don't know that there exists a precise mapping between body organs and psalm chapters. This is knowledge that was transmitted orally among the Sephardic Kabbalists. Psalm 6 for eyes — not because something about eyes is written there, but because the internal structure of the psalm, the way the word connections rise, creates a vibration that exactly matches the heavenly component of the vision system. Psalm 9 is suitable for children because it is said at the pitch of the little Satan's head — the high frequency in which children speak.

There is also a secret of timing: Psalm 6 is strongest between sunset and the appearance of stars — when the inner light of the eyes is most active. Psalm 41 is most effective in early morning hours, when the body's natural healing power is at its peak. This is not science — this is deep wisdom of how time and words and body integrate together in one great dance of healing.

questions.question: Which psalm is specifically designated for healing eye diseases?
questions.explanation: Psalm 6 is the designated chapter for healing eyes — both physical and the 'inner eyes' of the soul.

questions.question: Which verse expresses the secret of complete surrender to the Creator?
options: The Lord is my shepherd, I shall not want
Into your hand I commit my spirit
The Lord is a shield about me
I will give thanks to the Lord with my whole heart
questions.explanation: The verse 'Into your hand I commit my spirit' from Psalm 31 expresses complete surrender and release from the need to control everything.

questions.question: Which psalm did mothers say over the bed of a sick child?
questions.explanation: Psalm 9 — 'I will give thanks to the Lord with my whole heart' — is the chapter for healing a sick child according to Ashkenazi sages' tradition.

questions.question: What is the connection between kindness and healing in Psalm 41?
options: Kindness creates kindness — one who gives merits to receive
questions.explanation: The cosmic principle: 'Blessed is he who considers the poor' — one who has compassion on others merits that they have compassion on him.

questions.question: When is Psalm 6 strongest according to secret tradition?
options: Between sunset and the appearance of stars
In the middle of the night
questions.explanation: Between sunset and the appearance of stars — when the inner light of the eyes is most active.

closing.summary: We learned that Psalms are not just prayers but keys to healing and direct lines to protective forces. Psalm 6 heals eyes and soul, Psalm 9 saves sick children, Psalm 41 brings general healing through the principle of kindness. Psalms 18, 31 and 3 are our protectors from all evil and danger.
closing.wisdom: Kindness creates kindness — when you give healing, you receive healing.
closing.nextPreview: In the next lesson we will meet the psalms of livelihood and abundance — how holy words open gates of financial blessing.

faq.question: Can I read healing psalms for someone else?
faq.answer: Yes, especially for children or close family members. Pure intention and love connect to the healing frequency.

faq.question: How many times should I read each psalm?
faq.answer: The minimum is once with intention. In urgent situations — three or seven times in deep prayer.

faq.question: Do protection psalms also work for mental issues?
faq.answer: Absolutely. Worries, fears and stress are considered 'internal enemies' that the psalms address.

faq.question: What's better — reading from memory or from the book?
faq.answer: From the book is preferable for concentration, but in emergency — from memory with heart's intention is most important.

seo.title: Healing and Protection Psalms - Psalms 6, 9, 41 for Healing | Maktuba
seo.description: Learn how Psalms heal eyes, children and soul. Protection psalms from fears, enemies and evil eye. Secret tradition from Ashkenazi and Sephardic sages
